skill.name: personal-statement
skill.description: Use when writing medical school personal statements, residency application essays, fellowship statements, or graduate school admissions essays. Crafts compelling narratives highlighting clinical experiences, research achievements, and career motivations for healthcare education applications.
skill.summary: Crafts compelling personal statements for medical school, residency, fellowship, and graduate school applications in healthcare fields.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 41 potential issues across 4 files, but all findings are false positives. The 6 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections matched medical abbreviations (e.g., 'MD' in AMCAS) and narrative text, not actual cryptography. The 30 'backtick execution' detections matched markdown code formatting in SKILL.md, not shell execution. The 2 'hardcoded URL' detections reference a source attribution link. The 4 'system reconnaissance' detections matched documentation about reproducible output. The Python script (scripts/main.py, 33 lines) is a simple template generator with no network access, credential handling, or command execution. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in any file.

content.user_title: Write Compelling Medical School Personal Statements
content.value_statement: Applicants to medical and healthcare programs struggle to craft personal statements that stand out. This skill guides you through writing compelling narratives that highlight clinical experiences, research achievements, and career motivations.
actual_capabilities: Generates structured personal statement outlines with section-by-section guidance for medical applications
Frames clinical and research experiences using the STAR method for maximum narrative impact
Optimizes statement length to meet program-specific character limits including AMCAS and ERAS
Adjusts tone and identifies common cliches to strengthen personal narratives
Provides program-specific quality checklists for medical school, residency, and fellowship applications
Offers a reproducible workflow with explicit assumptions, error handling, and fallback instructions
limitations: Does not write a complete personal statement from scratch without user-provided experiences and goals
Requires users to supply their own clinical experiences, background details, and career motivations
Cannot verify factual accuracy of user-provided experiences, credentials, or achievements
The packaged Python script is a basic template generator that does not include the full feature set shown in code examples

use_cases.title: Medical School Applicant
use_cases.description: Pre-med students craft AMCAS personal statements that highlight clinical experiences and motivation for medicine within the 5,300 character limit.
use_cases.target_user: Pre-medical students applying to MD and DO programs

use_cases.title: Residency Candidate
use_cases.description: Medical graduates write specialty-specific personal statements for ERAS residency applications that connect clinical rotations to career goals.
use_cases.target_user: Fourth-year medical students applying to residency programs

use_cases.title: Fellowship Applicant
use_cases.description: Physicians develop focused fellowship statements that demonstrate subspecialty commitment, research contributions, and leadership experience.
use_cases.target_user: Residents and physicians applying to subspecialty fellowship programs

prompt_templates.title: Brainstorming Personal Statement Ideas
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me brainstorm ideas for my medical school personal statement. I have experience in [clinical volunteering] and [research]. My motivation for medicine is [motivation].
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner exploring narrative angles for the first time

prompt_templates.title: Structuring a Statement Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a structured outline for my residency personal statement in [specialty]. My key experiences are: 1) [experience one], 2) [experience two], 3) [experience three].
prompt_templates.scenario: User with defined experiences seeking organized structure

prompt_templates.title: Framing a Specific Experience
prompt_templates.prompt: Frame my experience of [specific experience] for a [program type] personal statement. I want to emphasize my [skill or quality] and connect it to my career goal of [goal].
prompt_templates.scenario: User polishing how a single experience is presented

prompt_templates.title: Optimizing for Character Limits
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my personal statement draft below and optimize it for the [AMCAS or ERAS or fellowship] character limit of [number]. Preserve all key experiences while eliminating redundancy. [paste draft]
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user finalizing a statement for submission

output_examples.input: Help me write a personal statement opening about my experience volunteering at a free clinic.
output_examples.output: Here is a structured opening for your personal statement. Opening hook: Describe a specific patient moment that crystallized your commitment to medicine. Reflection: Connect the clinical experience to your understanding of healthcare disparities. Transition: Bridge this experience to your motivation for pursuing medicine as a career.

output_examples.input: Review my personal statement for common cliches and weaknesses.
output_examples.output: I reviewed your statement and found these areas to improve. Replace the phrase 'I have always wanted to be a doctor' with a specific moment that shaped your decision. Add personal reflection after listing your research experience to show what you learned. Reduce the introduction by approximately 200 characters to stay within AMCAS limits.

best_practices: Provide specific clinical experiences and personal anecdotes rather than generic statements about helping people
Research your target program values and tailor your statement to connect your experiences with their mission
Use the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result) to frame each significant experience with concrete details and reflection
anti_patterns: Starting with 'I have always wanted to be a doctor since childhood' or similar universal cliches
Listing achievements and experiences without personal reflection on what you learned from each one
Writing the same generic statement for every program without tailoring to specific program values and specialties

faq.question: What types of personal statements can this skill help with?
faq.answer: This skill supports medical school (AMCAS), residency (ERAS), fellowship, and graduate school applications in healthcare fields.

faq.question: Do I need to provide my own experiences and background?
faq.answer: Yes, you must provide your clinical experiences, research background, career goals, and personal motivations. The skill helps structure and refine your content.

faq.question: Does this skill write the entire personal statement automatically?
faq.answer: No, it guides you through the writing process with outlines, framing techniques, and editing support. You provide the content and the skill helps shape it.

faq.question: What character limits does this skill understand?
faq.answer: The skill understands AMCAS (5,300 characters), ERAS (varies by specialty), fellowship (1 to 2 pages), and general graduate school application limits.

faq.question: Can this skill help if I am a re-applicant or career changer?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes guidance for re-applicant strategies and career changer narratives to help frame non-traditional paths effectively.

faq.question: How do I run the packaged Python script?
faq.answer: Run 'python scripts/main.py' after reviewing the configuration. The script generates a template-based personal statement that you can customize further.
